title: alliaria richii
intro: Alliaria richii, also known as the rich's garlic-mustard or rich's mustard, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family. It is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. The plant is rich in compounds with medicinal properties, including isothiocyanates, which have been shown to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. Rich's garlic-mustard can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function.
